vt_requirements.note: Every acceptance criterion or truth-state-advancing claim must record these four V&T fields.

title: C-RSP Build Contract — TLC Workbench Accessibility + Neurodivergent-First UX
goal: Make accessibility and neurodivergent-first UX release-gating requirements for both Workbench surfaces.
objective: Require keyboard-first, low-ambiguity, interruption-safe, adjustable, and auditable accessibility behavior in both the TUI and Web UI before verified release claims are allowed.
required_outputs: Keyboard-completable core flows in both surfaces.
Reduced motion, density, typography, focus mode, and recovery preferences.
Manual human accessibility and cognitive-load review artifacts.

acceptance_criteria.verification: Interaction tests or audits show every required flow can be completed without mouse-only input.

acceptance_criteria.statement: Reduced motion mode exists and is test-covered.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Tests and configuration evidence prove reduced motion can be enabled and affects both surfaces.

acceptance_criteria.statement: Density and typography preferences exist and persist.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Preference state persists across sessions and includes a dyslexia-friendly typography option.

acceptance_criteria.statement: Resume and recovery flow exists in both surfaces.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Recovery tests or audits show the operator can resume from the last meaningful state.

acceptance_criteria.statement: Automated accessibility audit passes the required thresholds.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Committed automated audit results meet or exceed the project accessibility threshold.

acceptance_criteria.statement: A manual audit artifact is committed and references real screens or flows.
acceptance_criteria.verification: A human-coded audit checklist or report exists and cites the reviewed surfaces.

acceptance_criteria.statement: Focus mode and explicit next-action affordance exist in both surfaces.
acceptance_criteria.verification: User-facing state and UI artifacts show low-clutter focus mode plus explicit next-action guidance.

halt_conditions.trigger: A required flow cannot be completed with keyboard-first interaction.
halt_conditions.resolution: Close the keyboard accessibility gap and re-run the accessibility checks.

halt_conditions.trigger: Recovery or resume support is absent for one of the required surfaces.
halt_conditions.resolution: Implement interruption-safe recovery and verify it in both surfaces.

halt_conditions.trigger: Automated accessibility audit artifacts are missing for a release-gated claim.
halt_conditions.resolution: Generate and commit the automated accessibility audit artifacts before proceeding.

halt_conditions.trigger: The manual accessibility or cognitive-load review artifact is missing.
halt_conditions.resolution: Complete and commit the manual accessibility review artifact.

halt_conditions.trigger: Required neurodivergent-first controls or visibility patterns are missing.
halt_conditions.resolution: Add the missing support controls and verify them with tests or audits.

evidence_requirements.description: Automated accessibility evidence for both Workbench surfaces.
Manual accessibility and cognitive-load review evidence.
